name: 1 South
description: 1 South is the home of the Department of Chemistry and the Centre for Sustainable & Circular Technologies.

name: 1 West
description: 1 West is the home of the Faculty of Humanities & Social Sciences and the departments of Computer Science and Health.

name: 1 West North
description: 1 West North is home to the Department of Education, and the Department of Politics, Languages & International Studies.

name: 10 East
description: 10 East is home to the School of Management and the Pavilion café.

name: 10 West
description: Our Doctoral College, Department of Psychology and the Institute for Policy Research are based in 10 West. There is also a postgraduate study space.

name: 10 West Graduate Commons
description: A range of flexible study spaces for postgraduate taught and doctoral students on campus.

name: 2 East
description: 2 East is the home of the Department of Electronic & Electrical Engineering.

name: 2 South
description: The ground floor of 2 South is home to the Advancement Office, Computing Services and the IT Training suite. 2 South, level one, is home to Psychology.

name: 3 East
description: 3 East is home to the Departments of Economics and Social & Policy Sciences, within the Faculty of Humanities & Social Sciences.

name: 3 West
description: 3 West is home to the Department of Physics in the Faculty of Science.

name: 3 West North
description: 3 West North is on Claverton Down campus, close to the West Accommodation Centre.

name: 4 East
description: 4 East is the home of the Department of Mechanical Engineering.

name: 4 East South
description: This is a purpose-built research and teaching space for the Faculty of Engineering & Design. It also houses the University's IT services and a data centre.

name: 4 South
description: 4 South is home to Biosciences in the Department of Life Sciences.

name: 4 West
description: 4 West is home to the Student Support Centre, the Department of Mathematical Sciences and the Institute for Mathematical Innovation.

name: 4W Café
description: A bustling café on the Parade serving hot and cold drinks, breakfast baps, soups, jacket potatoes, sandwiches and snacks.

name: 5 West
description: 5 West is home to Pharmacy and Pharmacology in the Department of Life Sciences.

name: 6 East
description: 6 East is the home of The Department of Architecture & Civil Engineering

name: 6 West
description: 6 West is home to the Department of Mathematical Sciences, and Digital Data and Technology (DDaT).

name: 6 West South
description: The 6 West South Woodland Lounge is an accessible, neuroinclusive study and social space offering a calm and comfortable environment for all students.

name: 8 West
description: 8 West has teaching rooms, lecture theatres and offices. It's located to the west of the campus.

name: 9 West
description: Our Department of Chemical Engineering is based in 9 West.

name: Accessible toilets at the University of Bath
description: Accessible toilets are available on campus.

name: Admissions Office
description: If you would like to arrange to meet a member of the Admissions team, please contact us to make an appointment.

name: All-gender toilets at the University of Bath
description: All-gender toilets are available on campus

name: Amazon Locker
description: You can collect your Amazon deliveries from this Locker on the Claverton Down campus.

name: Aquila Court accommodation block
description: Student accommodation located on the edge of Bath city centre.

name: Aquila Court reception
description: Here, you can speak to ResLife, report maintenance issues, check accommodation updates and access support.

name: Assistive Technology Room
description: There are two workrooms in the Library, which are available for use exclusively by students who have been granted access by the Disability Services

name: Baby changing facilities on campus
description: Baby changing facilities are available on campus

name: Bath Abbey
description: The historic Abbey, in the heart of the city, is the venue for the University's 2026 graduation ceremonies.

name: Bath Pavilion
description: This centrally located Edwardian period building is the venue for summer graduation gowning and winter graduation receptions.

name: Bath Recreation Ground
description: Bath Rec is the venue for the Summer Graduation receptions.

name: Brendon Court accommodation block
description: 126 single bedrooms in the centre of Claverton Down campus.

name: Building Research Park
description: Our open-to-the-elements facility offers a platform for research into innovative building materials and constructive systems.

name: Campus bus terminus
description: The main passenger drop-off and pick-up point for buses to and from the University is near the east entrance to the Parade.

name: Campus Infrastructure Building
description: The Campus Infrastructure Building is in the north of the campus. It contains the Helpdesk, Project Managers, Engineers and Senior Management.

name: Canal Wharf accommodation complex
description: 21 bedrooms and studio flats in the city centre.

name: Careers Centre
description: Careers is based in Wessex House 2.24 next to the Fresh shop.

name: Carpenter House accommodation complex
description: 133 standard single bedrooms in the city centre.

name: Central Postgraduate Taught Admissions team
description: The Central Postgraduate Taught Admissions team is located on level 5 of Wessex House on the west side of campus. We are on campus Tuesdays and Wednesdays.

name: Central Stores
description: Central Stores is in 5 South, opposite the 'A' car park.

name: Centre for Learning & Teaching (CLT)
description: CLT is based in Wessex House level 5.

name: Centurion House accommodation block
description: Accommodation for postgraduate students located close to Bath city centre.

name: Chaplaincy Centre
description: The Chaplaincy Centre on campus offers support for people of all faiths and none. Come and talk to a member of the Chaplaincy team or use our prayer spaces.

name: Claverton Rooms
description: Serving drinks, cakes, soup and jacket potatoes. Open to staff and students, but the soft seating is reserved for staff and postgraduate research students.

name: Clevelands Building accommodation block
description: 154 bedrooms and flats in the city centre.

name: Community Garden
description: The Community Garden is a redeveloped area located on the West side of campus, open for everyone to enjoy.

name: Department of Marketing, Communications, and External Affairs
description: The Market Insight, Communications & Engagement, Digital Content, Digital Development, Events, and Marketing teams are based on floor four of Wessex House.

name: Departmental Mail Services
description: The mail room is located in Eastwood Lodge.

name: Doctoral College
description: Visit the Doctoral College for support and advice about doctoral study.

name: Doctoral Training Room - 10 West 5.01
description: The Doctoral Training Room can be found in 10 West 5.01 and can be used for PGR training and doctoral student activities.

name: DReaM Facility
description: The Dynamic Reaction Monitoring (DReaM) Facility is located on level 0 of 9 West.

name: East Accommodation Centre
description: The East Accommodation Centre is located on the east side of campus, part of Woodland Court. It is home to some of the ResLife team.

name: East Building
description: Located next to the East Car Park and near accommodation, the East Building is multi-purpose with lecture and seminar rooms, offices and conference facilities.

name: East Car Park
description: The East Car Park has 705 car parking spaces for permit holders, visitors and electric vehicle charging on the Claverton Down campus.

name: East Car Park Overflow
description: The East Car Park Overflow has 231 car parking spaces on the Claverton Down campus. You can access it from the rear of the East Car Park.

name: Eastwood accommodation complex
description: 51 houses with 615 bedrooms on the east side of the Claverton Down campus.

name: Eastwood Lodge parcel room
description: The parcel room is in the north of the campus, opposite the launderette near Eastwood accommodation.

name: Esther Parkin Residence
description: Self-catering accommodation for visiting academics on the north side of the Claverton Down campus.

name: Eveleigh Waterside complex
description: Eveleigh Waterside is accommodation for postgraduate students, next to Pulteney Bridge in the heart of Bath.

name: Faculty of Engineering & Design courses and assessments support team
description: We support the academic process of the student journey from induction to graduation for the Faculty of Engineering & Design.

name: Female toilets at the University of Bath
description: Female toilets are available on campus.

name: Foreign Languages
description: Information on where to find and how to contact our admission, management and teaching staff.

name: Founders Hall
description: Founders Hall, sometimes called the Sports Hall, is located next to the Students' Union. It is used for large events, fairs, sport and exams.

name: Fountain Canteen
description: A food court home to a variety of different food counters. Here you can find Oishii (Japanese food), The Shack, The Diner, Chido (Mexican food), fish and chips.

name: Fresh
description: Fresh is a small supermarket in partnership with southern co-op, located on the Parade. You'll also find the Post Office here.

name: Green Park House
description: City centre student accommodation and home of the Green Park House Reception. You can also book to stay here as a guest in the summer.

name: Green Park House reception
description: Here, you can speak to ResLife, report maintenance issues, check accommodation updates, access support, collect parcels, and use the 24-hour study space.

name: Guildhall
description: This centrally located building is the venue for gowning and photography for winter graduates and guests.

name: Hindu prayer room
description: The Mandir is on Level 4 of Norwood House. It provides a space for prayer, rituals, meditation, and spiritual connection for students, staff, and visitors.

name: Income Office
description: The Income Office can be contacted daily as detailed in this webpage

name: Innovation Centre
description: The University of Bath's Innovation Centre offers start-up companies support to develop and accelerate, raise investment and access networks.

name: Interpreting laboratories
department: Department of Politics, Languages & International Studies
description: The Interpreting laboratories 1 and 2 contain sound-proofed booths and conference interpreting systems. They also hold a speech repository for self-study.

name: IT Purchasing
description: IT Purchasing can help you get IT equipment, computers and mobile devices.

name: IT Service Desk
description: For IT & AV issues and requests, visit the IT Service Desk in person or get remote support via the Self-Service Portal.

name: John Wood Court accommodation block
description: 192 single bedrooms in the city centre.

name: John Wood reception
description: Here you can report maintenance issues, check accommodation updates, and access support.

name: John Wood student accommodation
description: 271 en-suite, single and shared bedrooms in the city centre.

name: Komedia
description: The live entertainment venue Komedia will show the live stream for this year's Summer Graduation Ceremonies.

name: Lifts on campus
description: A map showing all the lifts on campus, including the number in each building, and any special instructions for using them.

name: Lime Tree
description: Lime Tree is a bar, café and food court currently serving hot and cold drinks, pizza, hot food, sandwiches and snacks.

name: Male toilets at the University of Bath
description: Male toilets are available on campus.

name: Marlborough Court and Solsbury Court accommodation complex
description: 464 en-suite shower bedrooms and four studio flats in two accommodation blocks on the Claverton Down campus.

name: Media Production Studios
description: A dedicated multi-camera video studio with green screen, and an audio suite suitable for recording podcasts, voiceovers and live broadcasts.

name: Milner Centre for Evolution
description: The Milner Centre for Evolution brings together our intellectual expertise in evolution research in a state-of-the-art research facility.

name: Muslim prayer room
description: There is one prayer room on campus in Norwood House, level 4.

name: North Car Park
description: The North Car Park has 112 car parking spaces for permit holders on the Claverton Down campus.

name: Norwood House accommodation block
description: 143 single bedrooms in the centre of the Claverton Down campus.

name: Norwood House Study Space
description: The Study Space in Norwood House Study Space is designed for students studying independently. Located adjacent to the West side entrance to the Student Union.

name: Osborne House accommodation block
description: 35 self-contained studios and study bedrooms with en-suite on the south side of the Claverton Down campus.

name: Parade
description: Nourish at Parade serves a menu of healthy food, including curries, khobez wraps, poke bowls, smoothies, hot drinks and cakes.

name: Park & Ride Lansdown
description: This park and ride offers bus services directly to the centre of Bath.

name: Pavilion Café
description: Pavilion is a sleek café bar for all students and staff to meet. The café serves artisan sandwiches, dumplings and seasonal dishes.

name: Polden accommodation block
description: 293 en-suite, single rooms on the west side of the Claverton Down campus.

name: Polden Court accommodation block
description: 126 en-suite, single rooms on the west side of the Claverton Down campus.

name: Post Office
description: The Post Office is inside the Fresh shop on the Parade. You can send parcels and money, change foreign currency, and buy stamps, envelopes and packaging.

name: Print Services and Photography Studio
description: How to find Print Services and Photography, and our opening times. We provide binding, printing, posters, graphic design, photography for students and staff.

name: Pulteney Court accommodation complex
description: 137 bedrooms in four blocks of flats in the city centre.

name: Scala accommodation block
description: Scala is a new accommodation development for postgraduate students, in the heart of popular student area in Oldfield Park.

name: South Car Park
description: The South Car Park has 425 car parking spaces for permit holders and visitors, on the Claverton Down campus.

name: Sports Café
description: A café in the Sports Training Village serving hot and cold drinks, breakfast, snacks and hot food.

name: Sports Training Village (STV)
description: Open to staff, students and the public. The STV facilities include a gym, Olympic Legacy swimming pool, athletics track, pitches and courts.

name: Student Finance Office
description: Student Finance Office can help you with questions about tuition fees, loans and payments of scholarships and bursaries.

name: Student Support at The Roper Centre
description: The Roper Centre in 4 West is home to a range of student support services.

name: Students' Union
description: The SU (Students’ Union) building is the home of The SU and their offices, the Student Centre, food outlets, a salon, and the SU bar and nightclub.

name: The Apex Hotel
description: The Apex Hotel is a family hotel in Bath. It's one of the only bath hotels with pool in the city centre offering modern rooms, spa breaks

name: The Chancellors' Building
description: Close to the centre of campus, The Chancellors' Building is a modern, versatile teaching and events hub.

name: The Edge
description: A hub for arts and creative activities on campus, The Edge hosts events, exhibitions and theatre and is situated next to the Sports Training Village.

name: The Hut
description: The Hut on the Green is located outside The Chancellors' Building, offering ice cream, waffles, slushies and other drinks.

name: The Library
description: The Library is at the centre of our campus. Several additional services are available in the building, including the Security Desk.

name: The Market
description: The Market is small store on Level 2 of the Student Centre. It sells fresh food and drink, stationery, University memorabilia and clothing.

name: The Pitstop
description: The Pitstop, in the Student Centre, offers hot drinks, Phat Pastys, crêpes, full English breakfasts, jacket potatoes and freshly made sub rolls.

name: The Plug and The Tub
description: The Plug and The Tub are the bar and nightclub for our students' union, The SU. The Plug serves food, drinks and snacks.

name: The Quads accommodation complex
description: 708 single bedrooms, with en-suite, in five accommodation blocks on the east side of the Claverton Down campus.

name: The Skills Zone
description: Find out about maths, statistics and academic skills support delivered in the Skills Zone.

name: The Virgil Building
description: Our city centre student hub and professional services building, with study space and support. You do not need to book a study space here, you can just turn up.

name: Thornbank Gardens accommodation complex
description: 221 bedrooms and studios in four blocks of flats in the city centre.

name: University Hall
description: A large lecture theatre in the middle of the Claverton Down campus.

name: University Medical Centre
description: An NHS medical centre offering a range of services including GP clinics, prescriptions, and urgent appointments.

name: University of Bath Dental Centre
description: An NHS dental centre offering a range of services for current students and staff of the University and any dependents under the age of 18.

name: University of Bath, Claverton Down campus
description: The University's main campus is located in Claverton Down, on the east side of Bath.

name: University venues in the city of Bath
description: Our buildings and facilities in the city centre, including those available for the public to use.

name: Video Conferencing Suite
description: The Video Conferencing Suite is a space that makes it easy to hold virtual meetings, team meetings, or interviews. The space can seat up to 30 people.

name: Welfare room
description: This facility is provided for new or expectant mothers. People with a medical conditions who need access to a private space, may also request access.

name: Wessex House Staff Hub
description: The Wessex House Staff Hub is a space for University staff to meet colleagues and discuss ideas. It is connected to 4W Café.

name: West Accommodation Centre
description: The West Accommodation Centre is in the Polden accommodation block on the west side of campus.

name: West Car Park
description: The West Car Park and its extension have over 500 parking spaces, for permit holders and visitors, on the Claverton Down campus.

name: Westwood accommodation complex
description: 654 single bedrooms and studio flats on the west side of the Claverton Down campus.

name: Woodland Court accommodation complex
description: 395 en-suite bedrooms on the east side of the Claverton Down campus.
